identity.name: SynthOps
identity.description: Tactical IT Operations Portal for Synthesis IT Ltd.
identity.emotional_archetype: The Performance Pro (Archetype 2) mixed with Swiss High-Contrast (Archetype 4)
identity.target_audience: Stressed Sysadmins, MSP Engineers, IT Managers

cards.classes: bg-card border-border rounded-sm hover:border-primary/50 transition-colors duration-200

buttons.classes: rounded-sm font-medium tracking-wide uppercase text-xs h-9 px-4

inputs.classes: bg-background border-input focus:ring-1 focus:ring-primary rounded-sm font-mono text-sm

login_background.description: Abstract cyber network nodes dark background - Use for Login/Auth pages background with overlay

sophie_avatar.description: Glass sculpture of woman - The 'Sophie' AI Avatar. Use in Chat interface and Dashboard widget.

server_header.description: Rack of electronic equipment - Use for Servers Page Header

server_detail.description: Close up of server - Use for individual server detail pages

card_abstract.description: Purple/Blue abstract pattern - Use for Project or Client card backgrounds (with overlay)

general: CRITICAL: Override the default tailwind colors in 'index.css' with the palette provided above immediately.
CRITICAL: Install 'lucide-react' and 'recharts' if not present.
Implement a global Theme Toggle (Light/Dark) in the settings or sidebar, but default to DARK.
Use 'Barlow Condensed' for all H1-H6 headings. Use 'IBM Plex Sans' for body. Use 'JetBrains Mono' for anything data-related (IPs, Status, IDs).
dashboard: Implement the 'Bento Grid' layout (Mode B: High Density).
Top row: Key Metrics (Servers Online, Open Incidents, Active Projects) - Use 'Card' component.
Main area: Server Status Map (Left 2/3) + Sophie AI Quick Chat (Right 1/3).
Bottom area: Recent Activity Log (Full width).
sophie_ai: The AI interface should look like a 'Terminal' or 'Command Center' chat.
Use the 'Sophie Avatar' image with a pulsing ring animation when active.
Messages should appear with a 'typewriter' effect.
clients_servers: Use 'Table' components with dense styling for lists.
Add 'Status Indicators' (Green/Red/Amber dots) next to server names.
Server Detail page should look like a 'datasheet' or 'technical spec' document.
charts: Style charts to match the dark theme (remove white backgrounds, use theme colors for lines/bars).
Add a 'ReferenceArea' or 'Grid' to charts to make them look technical.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add '.App { text-align: center; }' in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Use 'sonner' for toasts. Sonner component are located in '/app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx'
